description_long: This object is a vintage electronic control panel, featuring a rectangular body with a slightly angled front face. The front panel is equipped with multiple rotary knobs and push buttons arranged in vertical columns, flanking a central display area that appears to show labeled sections, possibly for different channels or functions. The display area is covered with a translucent material and is bordered by a metallic or plastic frame. The top of the device has a protruding cable, suggesting it is powered externally or connects to other equipment. The casing is made of dark-colored plastic or metal, with a robust and utilitarian design. The back of the object is mostly flat with a molded grip or mounting ledge, indicating it may be wall-mounted or handheld.
description: A vintage electronic control panel with multiple knobs, buttons, and a central display, housed in a dark, robust casing with a protruding cable.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the full array of knobs, buttons, and the central display area, as well as the cable emerging from the top.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the flat back, molded grip or mounting ledge, and the cable protruding from the top.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the back panel and mounting ledge, with minimal visibility of the control interface.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the right-side knobs and buttons, the central display, and the cable at the top.
